content.problemStatement:
Read problems statements in Russian hereChef's Girl Friend has given him a unique gift. She has given him a string **S**. Chef being a gentleman wants to return her gift in a unique way. He wants to break the string he has received into some number of substrings so that each substring is a palindrome. However he does not want break the string into too many substrings, otherwise the average size of his strings will become small. What is the minimum number of substrings in which the given string can be broken so that each substring is a palindrome.

Tips: Refer http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palindrome for the definition of a "palindrome"
constraints: All characters in the given string are upper case English alphabets
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the string, and the second line contains the string S itself.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum number of substrings in which the given string can be broken so that each substring is a palindrome.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The given string can be broken into "A" , "BCCB" , "D" , "A". It can be verified that you can't break the given string into less than 4 substrings such that each substring in a palindrome.
The given string is already a palindrome, so it can be broken into 1 substring.
The given string is already a palindrome, so it can be broken into 1 substring.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming. We create a 2D table dp where dp[i][j] is 1 if the substring from i to j is a palindrome, otherwise it is 0. We then create another array dp2 where dp2[i] is the minimum number of substrings in which the string from 0 to i can be broken so that each substring is a palindrome.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) per test case, O(T*N^2) total
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) — for the dp and dp2 arrays
